meta.name: Creamy Chipotle Sauce (Tex-Mex)
meta.subtitle: Smoky, tangy, lightly spicy sauce for tacos, fajitas, bowls, and dipping.

source.name: SSA Canonical Tex-Mex
notes: Designed as a multi-use condiment recipe with clear roles and optional heat scaling.
Uses canned chipotles in adobo; adjust heat by number of peppers and adobo amount.

summary: A fast, creamy chipotle sauce that balances smoky heat with tangy lime. Great on tacos, fajitas, burrito bowls, burgers, roasted veggies, and as a dip for fries or quesadillas.

notes: smoky_chipotle
tangy_lime
creamy
garlic_forward

optional.label: Food processor
optional.notes: Works if you don’t have a blender; blend longer for smooth sauce.

ingredients.label: Mayonnaise
ingredients.notes: For a lighter sauce, swap part of mayo for Greek yogurt.

ingredients.label: Plain Greek yogurt (optional)
ingredients.notes: Use as a partial swap for mayo or sour cream for a tangier, higher-protein sauce.

ingredients.label: Chipotle peppers in adobo, plus extra to taste
ingredients.notes: Start with 1 pepper for mild, 2 for medium, 3 for hot.

ingredients.label: Adobo sauce from the can
ingredients.notes: Adds smoky depth and heat; increase gradually.

steps.title: Add ingredients to blender
instructions: Add mayonnaise, sour cream (and Greek yogurt if using) to a blender jar.
Add 1–2 chipotle peppers, 1 tbsp adobo sauce (optional), lime juice, garlic, cumin (optional), smoked paprika (optional), salt, pepper, and honey (optional).
checks: All ingredients measured and in blender; start with fewer chipotles for mild

steps.title: Blend until smooth
instructions: Blend until completely smooth, scraping down the sides as needed.
Taste and adjust: add more chipotle/adobo for heat, more lime for brightness, or a pinch more salt to sharpen flavors.
checks: Sauce is smooth and pourable; chipotle evenly distributed

steps.title: Rest (optional) and serve
instructions: Use immediately, or refrigerate 15–30 minutes for flavors to meld.
Serve as a drizzle for tacos/fajitas/bowls, or as a dip for quesadillas and fries.
checks: Flavor is balanced: smoky + tangy + creamy; heat level matches preference

notes: Store in a sealed jar/container.
Stir before serving if any separation occurs.
Dairy/mayo-based sauces can separate after freezing and thawing.
If you must freeze, freeze in small portions and re-blend after thawing.
Keep refrigerated; do not leave at room temperature longer than 2 hours (1 hour if hot environment).
Use clean utensils to avoid contamination and extend shelf life.

batchPrep: Double the batch for meal prep week; label jar with date.
Use as a base sauce in bowls/wraps to reduce decision fatigue.

notes: For a thinner drizzle: blend in 1–2 tbsp water or milk.
For a thicker dip: increase sour cream/yogurt and reduce lime slightly.
Swap mayo to 1/2 cup and increase Greek yogurt to 1/2 cup.
Add an extra squeeze of lime and a pinch more salt to keep it punchy.
Use 1/2 chipotle pepper and 1 tsp adobo sauce.
Add 1/2 tsp smoked paprika for smoke without extra heat.
Use 3 chipotle peppers plus 1–2 tbsp adobo sauce.
Optional: add a pinch of cayenne or a splash of hot sauce.
Swap sour cream for a plant-based sour cream or thick cashew yogurt.
Keep mayo (egg-based) unless also avoiding egg.
